meta.title: ohmyhf — Unofficial Hugging Face desktop client
meta.description: ohmyhf is an unofficial Hugging Face desktop client for browsing, downloading, and managing the Hub — fast, local, and private. Open source, Apache-2.0, for macOS, Windows, and Linux.

hero.title: The Hugging Face desktop client that should have existed.
hero.subtitle: An unofficial, open-source app for browsing, downloading and managing the Hub — fast, local, private.
hero.badge: Apache-2.0 · macOS / Windows / Linux · No telemetry · English / 中文

pain.line: Twenty tabs. Endless reloads. A 300 GB cache folder you're afraid to open.

browse.title: Triple-pane browsing. Zero reloads.
browse.description: Sidebar, list, and live preview stay put while you switch Models, Datasets, Spaces, and Papers. Long lists stay smooth with virtualization; the right pane renders model cards, file trees, and metadata instantly — no new tabs, no full-page reloads.

command.title: Cmd+K. Keyboard-first Hub search.
command.description: One palette for search, filters, and sort. Narrow by task, library, license, or parameter count; rank by trending, likes, or downloads. Stay in flow without hunting through menus.

downloads.title: A download manager the browser can't match.
downloads.description: Queue jobs, run them in parallel, pause and resume, cap speed, and verify sha256 on finish — with system notifications when you're done. Files land in the standard Hugging Face cache layout, so transformers and the CLI just work.

cache.title: See your local cache. Reclaim the gigabytes.
cache.description: Scan your Hugging Face cache directory, inspect disk use per repo, and clean old revisions in one click. The web can't do this — ohmyhf can save you hundreds of gigabytes in ~/.cache/huggingface.

inbox.title: Watch inbox
inbox.description: Subscribe to users, orgs, and repos for updates and Daily Papers — with system notifications when something new lands.

compare.title: Model comparison
compare.description: Put 2–4 models side by side: parameters, license, and benchmarks in one glance so you can pick with confidence.

toolchain.title: Local AI toolchain
toolchain.description: Push GGUF into Ollama or LM Studio, send models to ComfyUI, and run an in-app inference playground — without leaving the desktop.

openSource.title: Open source
openSource.description: Apache-2.0 licensed. Read the code, fork it, audit it — the full repository is public on GitHub.

privacy.title: Privacy-first
privacy.description: Everything stays local in SQLite. No telemetry. OAuth tokens are stored with OS-level encryption — never in plaintext.

crossPlatform.title: Cross-platform
crossPlatform.description: Native builds for macOS, Windows, and Linux, with automatic updates so you stay current without hunting installers.

download.title: Download ohmyhf
download.notice: First release is on its way — watch the repo to get notified.
download.unsignedPrefix: macOS builds are unsigned for now — see how to open them in the

official.q: Is this an official Hugging Face app?
official.a: No. ohmyhf is an unofficial open-source project. It is not affiliated with, associated with, or endorsed by Hugging Face, Inc. “Hugging Face” is a trademark of Hugging Face, Inc., used here for identification purposes only.

free.a: Yes. The app is free and open source under the Apache-2.0 license. You can use it, study it, and contribute without paying a fee.

safety.q: Are my data and tokens safe?
safety.a: All app data stays on your machine in a local SQLite database. Tokens are stored with OS-level encryption, never in plaintext. There is no telemetry, and the source is public so you can audit what runs.

platforms.a: ohmyhf targets macOS, Windows, and Linux. Builds ship as dmg, NSIS installer, and AppImage / deb, with automatic updates once releases are published.

cache.q: Is the cache compatible with huggingface-cli and transformers?
cache.a: Yes. Downloads write to the standard Hugging Face cache layout. Models and datasets you fetch in ohmyhf work with huggingface-cli, transformers, and other tools that expect that layout.

contribute.a: We welcome issues, pull requests, and translations. Start with the contributing guide in the repository — it covers setup, coding standards, and how to propose changes.

footer.disclaimer: This is an unofficial open-source project, not affiliated with, associated with, or endorsed by Hugging Face, Inc. “Hugging Face” is a trademark of Hugging Face, Inc., used here for identification purposes only.
footer.notAffiliated: Not affiliated with Hugging Face, Inc.
